69f6ccbea8d8a47437e9598f.question: A Croatian-born critic who worked in this country used the term “novum” for scientifically plausible premises that ground works of science fiction. A theorist of postmodernism from this country coined the term “historiographic metafiction” to describe books like Ragtime. Characters from this country assume four “victim positions” according to a book that proposes “survival” as the central theme of its literature. A critic from this country drew on Shakespeare’s comedies to coin the term “Green World” and introduced methods of analysis based on modes, symbols, myths, and genres in a 1957 book. In a literary history of this country, the author of Fearful Symmetry named a fear of hostile landscapes in it termed “garrison mentality.”For 10 points, name this birth country of the author of The Anatomy of Criticism, Northrop Frye.
69f6ccbea8d8a47437e9598f.question_sanitized: A Croatian-born critic who worked in this country used the term "novum" for scientifically plausible premises that ground works of science fiction. Characters from this country assume four "victim positions" according to a book that proposes "survival" as the central theme of its literature. A critic from this country drew on Shakespeare's comedies to coin the term "Green World" and introduced methods of analysis based on modes, symbols, myths, and genres in a 1957 book. In a literary history of this country, the author of Fearful Symmetry named a fear of hostile landscapes in it termed "garrison mentality."For 10 points, name this birth country of the author of The Anatomy of Criticism, Northrop Frye.
69f6ccbea8d8a47437e9598f.answer: Canada(The first sentence refers to Darko Suvin. The second sentence refers to Linda Hutcheon. The third sentence refers to Survival: A Thematic Guide to Canadian Literature by Margaret Atwood.)

One of these places contains a monument with clasped hands that inspired “An Arundel Tomb.” For 10 points, Philip Larkin feels “awkward reverence” in a poem titled for what sort of place “going”?

The title vehicles are "closed like" components of these places in the first line of "Ambulances." A speaker wonders "what we shall turn" these places into in a poem that predicts "A shape less recognisable each week, / A purpose more obscure." "Echoes snigger briefly" in one of these "accoutred frowsty barns" when visited by a speaker who stops and takes off his cycle-clips. One of these places contains a monument with clasped hands that inspired "An Arundel Tomb." For 10 points, Philip Larkin feels "awkward reverence" in a poem titled for what sort of place "going"?
69f6ccbea8d8a47437e959b8.answer_sanitized: churches [accept cathedrals; accept confessionals; accept Chichester Cathedral; accept "Church Going"]

69f6ccc3a8d8a47437e95bfc.question: This thinker critiqued the idea of the resurrection of the body by imagining a soldier who cannibalizes an Iroquois who had cannibalized Jesuits, thus intermingling the atoms of many men in one body. 69f6ccc4a8d8a47437e95c7b.question: A woman from this city, Jeanne de Jussie (“zhahn duh zhoo-SEE”), wrote the “Short Chronicle” in which she argued that women were “truer Catholics than men.” In the early 17th century, the originally Luccan silk merchant Francesco Turrettini was influential in this city’s Grande Boutique consortium. The Citoyens (“see-twa-YANS”), Bourgeois, Habitants (“ah-bee-TAWNTS”), and Natifs were social orders in this city, whose bourgeoisie lost privileges after a 1798 foreign annexation. Isaac, a clockmaker from this city, regulated pendulums in Constantinople’s Topkapı Palace. Catherine Cheynel (“shay-NEL”) poured soup on invaders during a 1602 attack on this city commemorated by the Escalade. After the Battle of Solferino, an organization in this city was founded by Henri Dunant (“on-REE doo-NONT”). For 10 points, the establishment of the Red Cross in what city later led to its being the namesake of four humanitarian law “conventions”?

Isaac, a clockmaker from this city, regulated pendulums in Constantinople's Topkapı Palace. Catherine Cheynel ("shay-NEL") poured soup on invaders during a 1602 attack on this city commemorated by the Escalade. After the Battle of Solferino, an organization in this city was founded by Henri Dunant ("on-REE doo-NONT"). For 10 points, the establishment of the Red Cross in what city later led to its being the namesake of four humanitarian law "conventions"?
69f6ccc4a8d8a47437e95c7b.answer: Geneva [or Genève] (Isaac was the father of Jean-Jacques Rousseau.)

69f6ccc4a8d8a47437e95c94.question: Note to moderator: Read the answerline carefully. A 1960 essay laments that writers on this activity are not only “white middle-class Americans, but middle-brows as well.” The poem “Rose Solitude” by Jayne Cortez appears in Kevin Young’s anthology of poetry about this activity. 69f6ccc4a8d8a47437e95cc6.question: Diogenes of Apollonia thought that this thing “removes the sweet part” from one substance, explaining a natural phenomenon. A process driven by this thing caused the genesis of all other animals from fish according to Anaximander. The Tetrabiblos places this thing at the head of a hierarchy and credits it with the flowing of waters. Archelaus thought that a different entity must be “sunken” in the middle to explain an observation about this thing. “Daughters of” this thing guide the chariot in the Proem of Parmenides’s On Nature. Socrates calls this thing the “child of goodness” in an “analogy” from The Republic. Heraclitus thought that this thing was extinguished and rekindled each day, while Anaxagoras thought it was a mass of hot metal. For 10 points, the subject of the Allegory of the Cave is initially blinded by what celestial object?

Heraclitus thought that this thing was extinguished and rekindled each day, while Anaxagoras thought it was a mass of hot metal. For 10 points, the subject of the Allegory of the Cave is initially blinded by what celestial object?
69f6ccc4a8d8a47437e95cc6.answer: the Sun [or helios; accept sol; accept heliocentrism] (The first line refers to Diogenes of Apollonia’s theory of evaporation of water.)
